claims.claim_text: We present TTX, a Triton tuning framework that utilizes an XGBoost-based performance predictor to estimate kernel latency based on input shapes, tuning parameters, and IR-level features.

TTX achieves approximately 10% Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) on latency predictions for various operators, including matrix multiplication, batched matrix multiplication, and convolution across multiple GPUs.
Our framework selects promising candidates for further tuning, reaching about 80% of the best performance with the top-1 candidate and over 95% with the top-50 candidates.

We analyze cross-kernel generalization and identify a fundamental asymmetry in kernel transferability, showing that pointwise and normalization kernels transfer cleanly across kernel boundaries while compute-bound kernels do not.
